DOB: 07/03/1942 MEDICAL RECORD # :000117117 ACCOUNT NUMBER: 500753618 DATE OF ADMISSION: 02/15/2025 DATE OF SERVICE: 02/16/2025 REFERRING PHYSICIAN: Jayson L. Bautista APRN ADMITTING PHYSICIAN MD Minh H. Nguyen MD HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS: This is an 82-year-old female with history of stroke who presents with bilateral lower extremity weakness and gait disturbance. The patient also reportedly had acute emesis and diarrhea. The patient's previous CVA with right-sided deficits as well as chronic low back pain. The patient was seen by her home health and felt that she had declined and recommended acute rehab, PAST MEDICAL HISTORY: Significant for previous CVA with residual sided deficits, hypertension, and hypothyroidism. SOCIAL HEALTH HISTORY: Nonsmoker, nonalcoholic drinker. ALLERGIES: No known drug allergies. MEDICATIONS: Aspirin 81 mg daily, atorvastatin 10 mg daily, Lovenox 40 mg subcutaneously daily, gabapentin 600 mg daily, Keppra 250 mg daily, levothyroxine 88 mcg daily, pantoprazole 40 mg daily, and sertraline 50 mg daily. REVIEW OF SYSTEMS: A 12-point system review was performed, otherwise negative except noted on physical exam. PHYSICAL EXAM: VITAL SIGNS: Temperature 98.3, heart rate 72, respirations 18, and BP 132/61, HEENT: Normocephalic and atraumatic. Pupils equal and reactive. Oropharynx without injection. Report Request ID: 531952683 Page 1 of 3 Print Date/Time: 2/21/2025 10:59 C Feb/21/2025 10:15:33 AM Encompass Health 7029399744 5/21 Encompass Health Rehabilitation Hospital of Henderson Patient: KLEINER, BEVERLY G MRN: 117117 Admit Date: 2/15/2025 FIN#: 500753618 Discharge Date: DOB/Age/Gender: 7/3/1942 82 years Female Attending Phys: Nguyen MD,Minh H History and Physical NECK: Negative JVD or carotid bruits. LUNGS: Clear to auscultation. Negative rales, wheezes or rhonchi. HEART: Regular rhythm. ABDOMEN: Obese, soft, and nontender. Negative hepatosplenomegaly. EXTREMITIES: Positive pedal pulses. NEUROMUSCULAR EXAMINATION: Cranial nerves II-XII are grossly intact with no focal deficits. Manual muscle testing of the upper extremities are 5/5 and lower extremity 3-4/5. ASSESSMENT: 1. Generalized weakness/debility. 2. Bilateral lower extremity weakness with status post ground level fall on 02/01. 3. Previous CVA in 2015 with some residual weakness. 4. Hypertension. 5. Impaired mobility. 6. Impaired ADLs. DISCUSSIONS AND PLANS: The patient has impairment in bed mobility, transfers, unsteady gait and balance, easy fatigability and poor endurance and pain and overall loss of functional independence. Because of above medical problems and functional issues, it is my opinion the patient should be in acute rehab unit rather than a lower level of setting of care. Therefore, she will need multidisciplinary approach, three hours a day of therapy for five days a week, close medical supervision for 24 hours, and 24-hour rehab nursing care. There are no clinical significant differences between the patient's current status and information described in a preadmission screening document. There are NO safety or special Considerations that would likely preclude immediate implementation of an intensive rehabilitation program (3 hours per day, 5 days a week) or substantially influenced the plan of care Considering all the information above, it is my best judgment that this patient requires intensive rehabilitation therapy as described above in an inpatient hospital environment due to complexity of nursing, medical management and rehabilitation needs. Furthermore, this patient can reasonably be expected to participate in and benefit from an inpatient stay with an interdisciplinary team approach to the delivery of rehabilitation care under the direction and supervision of a rehabilitation physician. Other treatment plans: 1. Impairment with ambulation and mobility: Continue physical therapy. 2. Impairment of activities of daily living: Continue occupational therapy. 3. Gait and balance training. 4. Internal Medicine consultation. 5. Check laboratory data on a regular basis. 6. Will treat the patient as the clinical course unfolds. Continue with present medication regimen. REHABILITATION GOALS: Rehabilitation goals are to maximize patient's independence with ADLs, self care, mobility, communication skills and patient's medical status. Specific goals are to be established following completion of the assessment period. Goals include: 1. The patient is to be at a modified independent level with ambulation and mobility, and also with activities of daily living. 2. The patient is to be medically stable. REHABILITATION POTENTIAL/PROGNOSIS: Good, per the above stated goals. DISPOSITION: Home.
